title: Money Packing Codes
text: Money Packing Codes For Numeric fields that deal solely with amounts of money, you can specify a money packing code. If the DBMS supports a money data type, the database connector maps these codes to the appropriate money data type. Otherwise, the connector maps them to the appropriate numeric or double-precision data type, depending on the desired precision. See the relevant connector documentation for your DBMS for information about how your connector will map the various M packing codes. Table 1. Money Packing Codes Uniface Packing Code Explanation Uniface Data Types Numeric M1 Money: 8-byte integer Y M2 Money: double-precision floating point storage Y M4 Sybase internal money data type Y M6 Sybase small money format Y CAUTION: The M1 packing code is not the same as the I8 packing code, even though they both use 8-byte integer storage. The actual format of the data stored is incompatible. Scaling Uniface always applies a scaling of two (that is, two decimal places). You cannot specify a scaling when defining M packing codes. Table 2. Range of values for M packing codes Packing code Range of possible entries M1 +/- 922,337,203,685,477.58 M2 Exponent:(10 +/-38 ) Accuracy of mantissa ( DBMS-dependent): 16 digits Related Links Packing Codes for Numeric Data The Uniface Numeric and Float data types are associated with many packing codes. Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages.
